'''Nakaron''' was a [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] criminal who was wanted by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] for [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] and murder of New Republic personnel, assault on New Republic personnel, theft of New Republic property, reckless endangerment, and manslaughter. The reward for his capture was 60,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].

==Biography==
Nakaron was muscular and highly intelligent, but also extremely cruel and obdurate.<ref name="AJ1" />

===Outcast===
Nakaron was a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] and [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] like so many of his conspecifics. However, his success made him so self-assured that he started disregarding the class system that had been the cornerstone of [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] society for thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. He was eventually expelled from his home [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Forak]], and subsequently banned from entering many other cities on [[Trandosha/Legends|Dosha]], causing him to virtually be exiled from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]].<ref name="AJ1" />

Eventually, Nakaron was hired by [[Glorga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. He found out that one of Glorga's lieutenants, [[Lanish Ran]], was in reality a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agent, and decided to wait for the right moment to expose him. Ran, however, knew that Nakaron had found out. Nakaron knew that Ran had been planted as an agent, but he thought he was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] agent, because he had previously seen Ran while he was posing as an Imperial officer in Forak, and did whatever he could to prove absolute fealty to the Hutt. This went on for a long [[Time/Legends|time]], even after the establishment of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="AJ1" />

===The murder of Glorga===
During the [[Second Hollastin Insurrection]], when a band of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] assaulted Glorga's court, Nakaron thought his suspicions that Ran was an Imperial agent were confirmed. As the smugglers attacked, Nakaron took out two of them while Ran single-handedly took down the remaining eight. Nakaron was now convinced that Ran was an Imperial due to the formidable combat skills, which he believed were the result of Imperial military education. When Nakaron approach the Hutt with his theory a couple of weeks later, he was fired on the spot by the gangster who threatened to kill Nakaron should he ever show his face around again. Nakaron drew his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and killed the Hutt in response.<ref name="AJ1" />

===Arrest and escape===
Subsequently, he killed Ran in a blaster shootout that lasted 45 minutes, during which Nakaron managed to kill two dozen innocent passers-by. He was arrested within five minutes by another New Republic agent in the employ of Glorga—agent [[Thom Clessigan]]—who brought him aboard the [[Protector (shuttle)|''Protector'']] bound for a [[Prison/Legends|detention facility]] on [[Dles IV]]. Nakaron managed to hijack the ''Protector'' and kidnap agent Clessigan and the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] crew.<ref name="AJ1" />

==Physical appearance==
Nakaron was considered ugly, even by fellow Trandoshans. He had a sickly greenish complexion due to exposure to the [[Sandstorm/Legends|sandstorms]] of [[Varic]]. His skin appeared broken and unhealthy, because he had spent considerable time in the dry [[Desert/Legends|desert]] heat of [[Lorpfan]]. However, he was in great physical condition and incredibly strong and agile. He had almost no scars, unlike many of his [[Species/Legends|species]], with the exception of some mangled tissue caused by a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] accident.
